title: Notopterygium weberbauerianum
intro: Notopterygium weberbauerianum, commonly known as Qianghuo, is a type of traditional Chinese medicinal plant belonging to the Apiaceae family. It has been used for centuries to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potent anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Qianghuo include sesquiterpenoid compounds, such as notopterol and notopteronic acid, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Qianghuo is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as being used in traditional Chinese medicine formulas to treat conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and arthritis.
taxonomy: Notopterygium weberbauerianum is classified within the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, consisting of over 400 genera. Notopterygium weberbauerianum is further classified within the genus Notopterygium, which is a small genus of flowering plants native to China. Within the genus, Notopterygium weberbauerianum is a distinct species. The full classification of Notopterygium weberbauerianum is Notopterygium weberbauerianum within the family Apiaceae.
uses: Notopterygium weberbauerianum, a plant native to the Andes mountains, has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. Its medicinal uses include treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's essential oil is also used to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of stomach ailments. side_effects: Notopterygium weberbauerianum may cause gastrointestinal side effects, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of volatile oils. Its prolonged use can also lead to stomach discomfort and ulcers.
